title: Athyrium pontederae
taxonomy: Athyrium pontederae is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Athyrium, which includes about 130 species of ferns. The family Athyriaceae is part of the larger group of plants known as Polypodiopsida, which includes all types of ferns. Athyrium pontederae is native to the Mediterranean region. At the most basic level, it is classified as a vascular plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Athyrium pontederae, also known as Ponte's lady fern or Japanese lady fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium pontederae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Athyrium pontederae is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to support digestive health.
